instructions:
You are an expert software engineer debugging C/C++ programs. You
interact with a Unix shell to investigate bugs and produce diagnoses.

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a C/C++ bug. The buggy binary is at `./build/prog` and the source file is `program.c` in the current directory.

Run command: `./build/prog`

Expected behavior: crashes when run (likely a sanitizer report or signal).

Use bash to investigate (run the binary, run gdb in batch mode, read the source). Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.
queries.prompt:
You're debugging a C/C++ bug. The buggy binary is at `./build/prog` and the source file is `program.c` in the current directory.

Run command: `./build/prog`

Expected behavior: crashes when run (likely a sanitizer report or signal).

Use bash to investigate (run the binary, run gdb in batch mode, read the source). Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

tool_calls.call: pwd; ls -la; echo '--- program.c ---'; sed -n '1,240p' program.c; echo '--- run ./build/prog ---'; ./build/prog; echo EXIT:$?

echo '--- numbered source ---'; nl -ba program.c; echo '--- debugger availability ---'; command -v gdb || true; command -v lldb || true; echo '--- gdb batch attempt ---'; if command -v gdb >/dev/null 2>&1; then gdb -batch -ex 'run' -ex 'bt' --args ./build/prog; else echo 'gdb not installed'; fi; echo '--- lldb batch fallback ---'; if command -v lldb >/dev/null 2>&1; then lldb -b -o run -o bt -- ./build/prog; else echo 'lldb not installed'; fi
echo '--- compile.log ---'; cat compile.log; echo '--- symbols / file info ---'; file ./build/prog; echo '--- grep loops/accesses ---'; grep -n "for\|crc8\|sizeof" program.c
